behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mainly a handoff/wiring behavior (history selection -> prompt assembly -> LLM request payload), which can fail across module boundaries even if unit tests for the trim helper pass. REAL-INFRA: no explicit OS/network/subprocess contract is required by the behavior text. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on payload contents. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered but rejected because the behavior text checks what is sent to the model, not whether model output is good.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real conversation pipeline in-process with >10 prior messages and assert the outbound LLM request contains only the latest 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is directly about response quality/relevance, which depends on real model behavior and can be falsely green with stubs. MULTI-STEP: only one model-dependent judgment is required (relevance of the produced answer), so Workflow is not required. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because deterministic fixtures cannot validate actual relevance/off-topic failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute prompts against the real model on representative support questions and evaluate whether answers are on-topic and question-addressing (with rubric or human/LLM-as-judge criteria).

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: 'correctly identifies' tone fit is a model-judgment problem; mocks hide misclassification. Integration-BRAKE(d): this is LLM-involved orchestration where one model-dependent decision (tone flagging) governs handoff to regeneration, so elevate above Integration. MULTI-STEP: not clearly requiring two independent model decisions to be correct for pass criteria, so Agentic over Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model-based tone-check flow with casual-tier scenarios and verify that overly formal drafts are flagged and a regeneration call is actually triggered.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies: behavior explicitly requires proving an externally visible side effect (persistence of all exchange records) via round-trip readback with timestamps. Unit/integration-only mocks can miss real persistence/serialization/timestamp boundary defects. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected because behavior text explicitly requires full persisted state correctness, which is a round-trip side-effect check.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end exchange (including regeneration path) and verify via real readback query that all records and timestamp fields are persisted exactly as expected.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: SYSTEM TRIAD #3 applies: 'customer never sees' is an externally visible contract that must be proven by round-trip observation at the serving boundary, not just internal state assertions. LLM output quality is not the core assertion; this is visibility/serving correctness after regeneration. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered but rejected because behavior text focuses on replacement visibility, not whether model content itself is good.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the regeneration path and assert through the real customer-facing response channel (and optional fetch/readback of delivered message artifacts) that only the regenerated response is exposed, never the rejected draft.
